title: Go Module
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries a `go.mod` file that declares a Go module's identity, the minimum Go toolchain version, and its direct and indirect dependencies with their required minimum versions. Every Go module must have a `go.mod` at its root.

## When to use
Use `media:gomod;textable` when a pipeline node reads or updates Go module metadata — for example, a dependency update step running `go get`, a vulnerability scanner checking declared versions, or a build step that parses the module path to resolve imports.

## Notes
The `go.mod` format is managed by the `go` command and should not be edited by hand beyond simple version pins. Changes to `go.mod` are always accompanied by corresponding changes to [Go Checksum](https://capdag.com/browse/media:gosum;textable;list) (`go.sum`). See also [Go Module](https://capdag.com/browse/media:gomod;textable).
